content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes two floating-point numbers as input and calculates their product. Print the result with two decimal places.
constraints: The input numbers are floating-point numbers.
The product of the two numbers does not exceed the maximum limit of a float data type.
content.inputFormat: Two floating-point numbers separated by a space.
content.outputFormat: Print the product of the two numbers with two decimal places, prefixed by 'Product: ' and followed by a newline.

solution.approach: Read two floating-point numbers from the input, multiply them, and print the result with two decimal places.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) because the algorithm performs a constant number of operations regardless of the input size.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) because the algorithm uses a constant amount of space to store the input numbers and the result.

code.Python3:
num1, num2 = map(float, input().split())
product = num1 * num2
print(f"Product: {product:.2f}")
